data.title: TATA Consumer Products shares climb 1.40% in today's session
data.description:
Disclaimer This is an AI-assisted live blog with updates sourced from multiple news outlets and agencies Disclaimer

Shares of TATA Consumer Products experienced a positive movement in today's session, with the stock price increasing by 1.40% to reach Rs 1163.30. This rise reflects a favorable shift in investor sentiment towards the company.

TATA Consumer Products is part of the NIFTY 50 STOCKS, indicating its significance in the Indian stock market. The company's performance is closely watched by investors and analysts alike.

Financial Overview


A review of TATA Consumer Products' financial performance offers insights into its growth and stability. Here’s a summary of the company’s financial highlights:

Revenue


  • Mar 2025: Rs 4,608.22 Cr
  • Dec 2024: Rs 4,443.56 Cr
  • Sep 2024: Rs 4,214.45 Cr
  • Jun 2024: Rs 4,352.07 Cr
  • Mar 2024: Rs 3,926.94 Cr

The company has shown consistent revenue growth over the past year, with revenue increasing from Rs 3,926.94 Cr in March 2024 to Rs 4,608.22 Cr in March 2025.

Net Profit


  • Mar 2025: Rs 407.07 Cr
  • Dec 2024: Rs 305.91 Cr
  • Sep 2024: Rs 359.34 Cr
  • Jun 2024: Rs 314.15 Cr
  • Mar 2024: Rs 267.71 Cr

Net profit has also shown a positive trend, increasing from Rs 267.71 Cr in March 2024 to Rs 407.07 Cr in March 2025.

EPS (Earnings Per Share)


  • Mar 2025: 3.49
  • Dec 2024: 2.82
  • Sep 2024: 3.78
  • Jun 2024: 3.05
  • Mar 2024: 2.28

The EPS has increased from 2.28 in March 2024 to 3.49 in March 2025.

Annual Financial Performance


The annual financial performance of TATA Consumer Products provides a broader perspective on its financial health.

Key Figures


  • Revenue: Increased from Rs 11,602.03 Cr in 2021 to Rs 17,618.30 Cr in 2025.
  • Net Profit: Rose from Rs 993.79 Cr in 2021 to Rs 1,380.31 Cr in 2025.
  • EPS: Increased from 9.30 in 2021 to 13.06 in 2025.
  • BVPS: Increased from 169.33 in 2021 to 202.13 in 2025.
  • ROE: 5.90% in 2021 to 6.39% in 2025.
  • Debt to Equity: 0.05 in 2021 to 0.09 in 2025.

Cash Flow


Analyzing the cash flow provides further insights into the company's operational efficiency and financial management.

Cash Flow From


  • Operating Activities: Increased from Rs 1,656 Cr in 2021 to Rs 2,056 Cr in 2025.
  • Investing Activities: Remained negative, indicating ongoing investments.
  • Financing Activities: Fluctuated over the years, with a net positive in 2025.
  • Net Cash Flow: Showed variability, with a positive flow of Rs 205 Cr in 2025.

Balance Sheet


The balance sheet reflects the company's financial structure and stability.

Equities & Liabilities


  • Share Capital: Increased from Rs 92 Cr in 2021 to Rs 98 Cr in 2025.
  • Reserves & Surplus: Increased from Rs 14,442 Cr in 2021 to Rs 19,902 Cr in 2025.
  • Total Liabilities: Increased from Rs 20,255 Cr in 2021 to Rs 31,977 Cr in 2025.

Assets


  • Fixed Assets: Increased from Rs 4,539 Cr in 2021 to Rs 10,364 Cr in 2025.
  • Total Assets: Increased from Rs 20,255 Cr in 2021 to Rs 31,977 Cr in 2025.

Key Ratios


Various financial ratios provide insights into TATA Consumer Products' efficiency and profitability.

Margin Ratios (Mar 2025)


  • Gross Profit Margin: 15.16%
  • Operating Margin: 11.75%
  • Net Profit Margin: 7.83%

Return Ratios (Mar 2025)


  • Return on Networth / Equity: 6.39%
  • ROCE: 7.92%
  • Return On Assets: 3.99%

Leverage Ratios (Mar 2025)


  • Debt to Equity: 0.09
  • Interest Coverage Ratios: 9.21

Valuation


  • P/E (x):76.72
  • P/B (x):4.96
  • EV/EBITDA (x):37.25
  • P/S (x):5.63

Market Sentiment


According to Moneycontrol analysis as of 2025-05-12, the sentiment for TATA Consumer Products is very bullish. With the stock's current price at Rs 1163.30, TATA Consumer Products has demonstrated a positive trend in today's session, supported by consistent financial growth and a bullish market sentiment.

data.title: Nifty Midcap top gainers: Cochin Shipyard stock jumps 12%; Rail Vikas Nigam, Mazagon Dock shares up 9%
data.description:
Disclaimer This is an AI-assisted live blog with updates sourced from multiple news outlets and agencies Disclaimer

During today's session, the NIFTY MIDCAP 150 witnessed significant activity, with several stocks posting notable gains. Among the top gainers were Cochin Shipyard, Rail Vikas, and Mazagon Dock, showcasing strong performance within the index. The data reflects a bullish trend in specific sectors, driving these stocks to the forefront of market activity.

Cochin Shipyard led the pack with a gain of 12.11%, reaching a price of Rs 2,031.60. Following closely behind, Rail Vikas saw an increase of 9.2%, with its stock price climbing to Rs 410.85. Mazagon Dock also performed well, rising by 8.46% to reach Rs 3,451.60. Other notable gainers included SJVN, which increased by 6.11% to Rs 104.36, and BHEL, with a gain of 5.53% to Rs 259.26.

Financial Snapshot of Top Performers


A look at the financials of some of the top-performing companies reveals key insights into their recent performance. Here’s a summary based on available data:
CompanyRevenue (Mar 2024)Revenue (Mar 2025)Net Profit (Mar 2024)Net Profit (Mar 2025)EPS (Mar 2024)EPS (Mar 2025)
Rail VikasRs 6,714.01 CrNARs 435.34 CrNARs 2.29NA
Mazagon DockRs 3,103.65 CrNARs 654.33 CrNARs 32.44NA
BHELRs 8,260.25 CrNARs 476.03 CrNARs 1.41NA

Revenue Trends


Examining the revenue trends for these companies, we can observe the following:
  • Rail Vikas: Revenue has shown consistent performance over the years, with Rs 21,889 crore in March 2024. Quarterly data indicates Rs 4,567 crore in December 2024.
  • Mazagon Dock: The company's revenue stood at Rs 9,466 crore for March 2024, with a quarterly revenue of Rs 3,143 crore in December 2024.
  • BHEL: Annual revenue was reported at Rs 23,892 crore in March 2024, while the December 2024 quarter saw revenue at Rs 7,277 crore.

Net Profit Analysis


The net profit figures provide additional insight into the profitability of these firms:
  • Rail Vikas: Recorded a net profit of Rs 1,574 crore in March 2024, with Rs 254 crore in December 2024 quarter.
  • Mazagon Dock: Displayed a net profit of Rs 1,845 crore in March 2024, with Rs 768 crore in December 2024 quarter.
  • BHEL: The company's net profit was Rs 218 crore in March 2024, and Rs 116 crore in the December 2024 quarter.

According to Moneycontrol analysis, the sentiment analysis is Very Bullish as of 2025-05-13.

The NIFTY MIDCAP 150's top gainers included Cochin Shipyard at Rs 2,031.60 (12.11%), Rail Vikas at Rs 410.85 (9.2%), Mazagon Dock at Rs 3,451.60 (8.46%), SJVN at Rs 104.36 (6.11%) and BHEL at Rs 259.26 (5.53%).

data.title: Celebi shares crash 10% in Istanbul as India revokes security clearance for Turkish firm
data.description:
Celebi shares crash 10% in Istanbul as aviation watchdog revokes security clearance

Shares of Celebi Hava Servisi AS crashed 10% in Istanbul on May 16 a day after aviation watchdog BCAS revoked the security clearance of the firm's India unit, Celebi Airport Services India.

BCAS had revoked the security clearance of ground handling company Celebi Airport Services India with immediate effect in the “interest of national security”.

The move by the Bureau of Civil Aviation Security came against the backdrop of Turkiye’s backing for Pakistan and its condemnation of India’s recent strikes on terror camps in the neighbouring country and Pakistan-occupied Kashmir (PoK) under ‘Operation Sindoor’. Pakistan had also used Turkish drones on a large scale in the military conflict with India.

“… the security clearance in r/o Celebi Airport Services India Pvt Ltd is hereby revoked with immediate effect in the interest of national security,” the BCAS said in an order. The security clearance under the category ‘Ground Handling Agency’ was approved by the BCAS in November 2022.

The stock was under pressure in the past few days amid clamour for action against the firm.

On May 16, the stock fell 222 points, or 10%, to trade at 2,002, extending its losses to nearly 30% in four sessions.

Çelebi Aviation India rejected allegations regarding the company’s ownership and operations in India and reaffirmed its long-standing commitment to the country’s aviation sector.

“We are not a Turkish organisation by any standard and adhere fully to globally accepted practices of corporate governance, transparency, and neutrality, with no political affiliations or links to any foreign government or individuals,” it said in a statement.

In compliance with the BCAS directive, Delhi International Airport Limited (DIAL) formally ended its association with Çelebi entities responsible for ground handling and cargo operations at the Indira Gandhi International Airport (IGIA).

Following the termination, DIAL is working closely with existing service providers to ensure uninterrupted operations while safeguarding employee welfare, a DIAL statement said.

Celebi Airport Services India, which is part of Turkiye-based Celebi, offers services at nine airports — Mumbai, Delhi, Cochin, Kannur, Bangalore, Hyderabad, Goa (GOX), Ahmedabad and Chennai. Celebi has been operating in India for the past 15 years.

According to Celebi’s website, its presence in India grew exponentially with three different entities.

With inputs from PTI
data.author: MoneyControl
